html:
Sequitor's Puzzle

Exclusive to:

“Paths are taken but once. Leap wisely, tarry not, or the abyss claims all.”

Sequitor's Puzzle is a puzzle that appears in one of the rooms of the Sanctum. Solving the puzzle will allow players to get to the next rooms of the Sanctum, including the room with the orange Fountain of Knowledge.

Contents

  • 1 Puzzle
  • 2 Sequitor
  • 3 Trivia
  • 4 Gallery

Puzzle

To activate the puzzle, players must pull a Lever, which causes multiple Sanctum Rocks to appear. Stepping onto the first rock starts the puzzle, after which two Sequitors join the board. Sequitors mirror the player’s movements and each rock can only be stepped on once by either the player or a Sequitor before collapsing. The objective is to reach the opposite side of the room. Sanctum Rocks collapse a few seconds after being stepped on, or immediately once the character standing on them moves away.

Completing the puzzle grants access to a Sanctum Waymark that leads to a room containing several Sanctum Statues and another Sanctum Waymark that leads to Purifier Room.

There are 3 different variants of the puzzle. Pulling the Lever can restart the puzzle or roll for different variants.

Sequitor

ActiveInactive

“They block your path. Are they following a pattern?”

-

Sequitor is a passive mob inside the Sequitor's Puzzle room. At first, they stand inactively on the side of the puzzle. When a player pulls the Lever and jumps onto the rocks, two of them will also hop on the rocks and mirror the player's movement.

Trivia

  • The Sequitor's Puzzle was added in the Ancient Echoes update.
  • The Sequitor and the Ancient Sentrypede share the same design.
  • Sequitor's name comes from Latin word "sequitur", which means "it follows".

Gallery

  • Sanctum Concept Art from Rhymes with Play stream.

To activate the puzzle, players must pull a Lever, which causes multiple Sanctum Rocks to appear. Stepping onto the first rock starts the puzzle, after which two Sequitors join the board. “How unoriginal!”
–Wilson
“Copycat!”
–Willow
“Stop copying Wolfgang!”
–Wolfgang
“It follows.”
–Wendy
“MOVEMENT PATTERN DETECTED: MIRRORING.”
–WX-78
“It follows a particular movement pattern.”
–Wickerbottom
“I knew it!”
–Woodie
“Bug off! Ugh, I sound like Wilson.”
–Maxwell
“Vile insect, thöu darest tö mimic my step?”
–Wigfrid
“Hey! No fair!”
–Webber
“Where are you going?!”
–Warly
“Where friend going?”
–Wormwood
“Hey, quit it!”
–Winona
“At least it waits its turn, but what's the pattern to discern?”
–Wortox
“Stop it, florp!”
–Wurt
“Hey, it moves when I move!”
–Walter
“Yes, they follow a pattern...”
–Wanda
“I'm glad it's just a statue.”
–Wilson
“Someone's obsessed with bugs.”
–Willow
“Wolfgang not afraid of bug statue.”
–Wolfgang
“A lifeless statue.”
–Wendy
“INERT”
–WX-78
“A statue of a large insectoid form.”
–Wickerbottom
“I don't like it either, Luce.”
–Woodie
“It's ugly.”
–Maxwell
“The insect immörtalized, yet unmöving.”
–Wigfrid
“We're glad it's just a statue!”
–Webber
“What a menacing statue!”
–Warly
“Friend sleeping”
–Wormwood
“I don't trust them statues.”
–Winona
“How do you do, statue?”
–Wortox
“Glurgh.”
–Wurt
“So creepy!”
–Walter
“There's something about it...”
–Wanda
“They block your path. Are they following a pattern?”

Sequitor is a passive mob inside the Sequitor's Puzzle room. At first, they stand inactively on the side of the puzzle. When a player pulls the Lever and jumps onto the rocks, two of them will also hop on the rocks and mirror the player's movement.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Sequitor's Puzzle was added in the Ancient Echoes update.
The Sequitor and the Ancient Sentrypede share the same design.
Sequitor's name comes from Latin word "sequitur", which means "it follows".

title: Sequitor's Puzzle
wikitext:
[[File:Sanctum Puzzle ingame.png|thumb|right|300px|Sequitor's Puzzle]]
{{Exclusivity|Don't Starve Together}}
{{Quote|Paths are taken but once. Leap wisely, tarry not, or the abyss claims all.}}
'''Sequitor's Puzzle''' is a puzzle that appears in one of the rooms of the [[Sanctum]]. Solving the puzzle will allow players to get to the next rooms of the Sanctum, including the room with the orange [[Fountain of Knowledge]].

==Puzzle==
To activate the puzzle, players must pull a [[Lever]], which causes multiple [[Sanctum Rocks]] to appear. Stepping onto the first rock starts the puzzle, after which two [[#Sequitor|Sequitor]]s join the board. Sequitors mirror the player’s movements and each rock can only be stepped on once by either the player or a Sequitor before collapsing. The objective is to reach the opposite side of the room. Sanctum Rocks collapse a few seconds after being stepped on, or immediately once the character standing on them moves away.

Completing the puzzle grants access to a [[Sanctum Waymark]] that leads to a room containing several [[Sanctum Statue]]s and another Sanctum Waymark that leads to [[Purifier Room]].

There are 3 different variants of the puzzle. Pulling the Lever can restart the puzzle or roll for different variants. 
{{clear}}
